content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to treat incomplete, unlabeled exploration traces of a standalone agent’s core decision-making as dynamically curateable memory segments, where curiosity-driven exploration rewards are weighted by how much the agent’s own unindexed memory gaps instead of just environmental novelty. This creates a self-reinforcing memory loop where the agent actively prioritizes learning to fill its own memory blind spots rather than only exploring generic novel environments, aligning the agent’s intrinsic drive directly to its long-term task performance.
